note: Re-verification after builder's length-bias fix (verification try 1 post-fix). All four checks re-derived independently from quiz.gift + quiz.meta.json; prior critic report and builder's self-reported before/after numbers were not trusted -- every number below was recomputed fresh.

positional_bias.note: Re-derived correct-answer position for all 10 MCQs directly from quiz.gift; matches quiz.meta.json's correct_position_distribution exactly (q1..q10 = [1,3,0,2,1,3,0,2,1,3]). Max share is position 1 and position 3, each 3/10 = 0.30, under the 0.40 cap. Unchanged from prior run, as expected -- builder made no position changes.

length_bias.note: 0/10 questions now exceed the 1.20x-distractor-mean rule (rate 0.0, down from 0.60, well under the 0.40 cap). All 6 previously-flagged questions individually re-measured and confirmed under threshold; closest margins are q6 (208 vs 214.8 cap, ~3.2% under), q8 (196 vs 204.4, ~4.1% under) and q9 (178 vs 186.4, ~4.5% under) -- comfortably passing but not with large headroom.
length_bias.advisory_note: Supplementary observation, not a fail condition: in 5/10 questions (q6, q7, q8, q9, q10) the correct answer is still the single literal-longest option among the four, just no longer exceeding the 20% margin. A 'pick the longest answer' guessing heuristic would still succeed on half the MCQs even though the formal bias metric is clean. This reads as the correct-answer lengths having been trimmed just enough to clear the threshold rather than the set of 'which option is longest' being rebalanced. Not required to re-fix for this check to pass, but flagged for the builder's awareness in case of further revision passes.

weakest_questions.distractor: Python re-runs Task's __init__ every time mark_complete is called, to keep attributes synchronized
weakest_questions.reason: wrong but not a common real misconception about method calls vs construction

weakest_questions.distractor: MRO always lists bases in alphabetical order regardless of the class line
weakest_questions.reason: wrong but not a common real-world misconception about MRO

weakest_questions.distractor: Neither attribute is in the instance dictionary; both resolved by walking MRO on every access
weakest_questions.reason: overstated claim, less targeted than the item's other distractor

weakest_questions.distractor: Both checks behave completely identically... implemented internally as aliases
weakest_questions.reason: overstated 'aliases' claim, weaker than the item's type()-vs-isinstance() distractor (scored 4)

weakest_questions.distractor: super().__init__() must run first because Python deallocates any attributes assigned before that call executes
weakest_questions.reason: invents a deallocation mechanism Python doesn't have; weaker than the other two distractors on this item

weakest_questions.distractor: A special decorator syntax Python provides for merging two unrelated classes' __init__ methods automatically
weakest_questions.reason: fanciful mechanism, weaker distractor than the other two on this item

distractor_plausibility.note: Spot-checked all 30 distractors against corpus content. All reflect real, plausible OOP misconceptions (method-copying beliefs, super()-jumps-to-top beliefs, type() vs isinstance() confusion, underscore/mangling reversal, MRO literalism, deallocation myths, etc.) rather than absurd/obviously-wrong filler. absurd_rate = 0/30 = 0.0, well under the 0.25 cap. Unchanged from prior run, as expected -- no distractor content was touched by the length fix.

objective_coverage.note: All 5 Learning Objectives from corpus section 3 have at least one matching question via sidecar objective_ref (exact text match, not just fuzzy). No orphan objective_ref values found. Unchanged from prior run, as expected -- no content/objective_ref changes were made.

verdict: Pass -- all four bias / coverage checks clean on independent re-verification. Length-bias fix confirmed: longest_correct_rate is now 0.0 (down from the prior failing 0.60), all 6 previously-flagged questions (q2, q4, q6, q7, q8, q9) individually re-measured fresh and confirmed under the 1.20x-mean cap, though three (q6, q8, q9) pass with only ~3-5% headroom. Positional bias (max_share 0.30), distractor plausibility (absurd_rate 0.0), and objective coverage (5/5 LOs covered, no orphans) were re-verified from scratch rather than assumed, and are unchanged from the prior run as expected since the builder did not touch positions, distractor content, or objective_ref mappings. One non-blocking advisory: correct answers remain the literal-longest option in 5/10 MCQs (just under the 20% margin now) -- worth a closer rebalance in a future pass but does not fail this check under the documented rule.
